SEARCH
Package jGameLib.math

Class Vec2

java.lang.Object
jGameLib.math.Vec2
public final class Vec2 extends Object
A class representing a pair of floating point coordinates or size. Vectors can be added to each other, scaled, and multiplied component-wise. Vectors are immutable, which means that they cannot be changed once constructed.

  • Field Details

    • x

      public final double x

    • y

      public final double y

    • zero

      public static final Vec2 zero
      The zero vector [0, 0]

  • Constructor Details

    • Vec2

      public Vec2(double x, double y)

    • Vec2

      public Vec2(double v)
      Constructs the vector [v, v]

    • Vec2

      public Vec2(Dimension d)
      Creates a Vec2 from a Dimension

    • Vec2

      public Vec2(Vec2i v)
      Creates a Vec2 from a Vec2i.
      Decimal places are truncated.

  • Method Details

    • add

      public static Vec2 add(Vec2 a, Vec2 b)
      Adds two vectors.
      add([x1, y1], [x2, y2]) = [x1 + x2, y1 + y2]

    • sub

      public static Vec2 sub(Vec2 a, Vec2 b)
      Subtracts two vectors.
      sub([x1, y1], [x2, y2]) = [x1 - x2, y1 - y2]

    • scale

      public static Vec2 scale(Vec2 p, double c)
      Scales a vector by a constant c.
      scale([x, y], c) = [c * x, c * y]

    • scale

      public static Vec2 scale(Vec2 p, double sx, double sy)
      Scales each component of a vector by sx and sy, respectively.
      scale([x, y], sx, sy) = [sx * x, sy * y].
      This is a shortcut for component-wise vector multiplication.

    • magnitude

      public static double magnitude(Vec2 p)
      Returns:
      The length of the vector sqrt(x * x + y * y)

    • distance

      public static double distance(Vec2 a, Vec2 b)
      Returns:
      The distance between two vectors, equal to magnitude(sub(a, b))

    • manhattanDistance

      public static double manhattanDistance(Vec2 a, Vec2 b)
      Returns:
      The manhattan distance between two vectors.
      manhattanDistance([x1, y1], [x2, y2]) = abs(x1 - x2) + abs(y1 - y2)

    • x

      public double x()
      Returns:
      The x-component of the vector

    • y

      public double y()
      Returns:
      The y-component of the vector

    • equals

      Overrides:

      Shorthand for add(Vec2, Vec2)

      public Vec2 minus(Vec2 other)
      Shorthand for sub(Vec2, Vec2)

      Shorthand for scale(Vec2, double, double)

      Shorthand for scale(Vec2, double)

      Shorthand for scale(Vec2, double, double)

    • times

      public Vec2 times(Vec2 scale)
      Shorthand for scale(Vec2, double, double)

    • magnitude

      public double magnitude()
      Equivalent to magnitude(Vec2)

    • distanceTo

      public double distanceTo(Vec2 other)
      Shorthand for distance(Vec2, Vec2)

    • manhattanDistanceTo

      public double manhattanDistanceTo(Vec2 other)
      Shorthand for manhattanDistance(Vec2, Vec2)

    • transform

      public Vec2 transform(DoubleUnaryOperator func)
      Applies the given function to both components of the vector and returns the resulting vector

    • transform

      public static Vec2 transform(Vec2i v, AffineTransform transform)
      Applies an AffineTransform to a vector

    • average

      public static Vec2 average(Vec2... v)
      Takes the average of the vectors

    • average

      public static Vec2 average(Vec2 v1, Vec2 v2, double w1)
      Equivalent to average(Vec2..., double...)({v1, v2}, {w1, 1 - w1}). When t is zero, the output is v2, and when t is 1, the output is v1

    • average

      public static Vec2 average(Vec2[] v, double[] w)
      Takes a weighted average of the vectors. The output is equal to 
      sum(v[i] * w[i]) / sum(w[i]).

    • interpolate

      public static Vec2 interpolate(Vec2 v1, Vec2 v2, double t)
      Linearly interpolates between two vectors When t is zero, the output is v1, and when t is 1, the output is v2. Otherwise, the result is a mix of the two vectors.

    • smoothInterpolate

      public static Vec2 smoothInterpolate(Vec2 v1, Vec2 v2, double t)
      Interpolates between two vectors using the MathUtil.smoothStep(double) function
